options: Keeping references to objects longer than needed so memory cannot be reclaimed

que: Why are memory leaks harmful on Android?
options: They increase memory pressure and can lead to jank, freezes, or kills
They have no effect because of garbage collection

que: Which long-lived reference commonly causes leaks in Android apps?
options: A stored `Activity` reference inside a singleton
A local variable inside a function

que: What do Android memory docs say about unnecessary running services?
options: They are one of the worst memory-management mistakes
They are required for all apps

que: Which tool helps inspect memory usage and leaks in Android Studio?
Which is a good practice to reduce leaks?
options: Unregister listeners and callbacks when they are no longer needed
Store every `View` in a static field
Keep services running after work completes

que: What is a common misconception about memory leaks?
options: Garbage collection will automatically fix leaked references
Android apps can run under memory pressure

que: Which context should not be stored casually in long-lived objects?
What should happen to a service when its work is done?
options: It should replace the application class

que: What is a practical sign that memory investigation may be needed?
options: Memory keeps growing unexpectedly over time
